creator: jacula@gmail.com
text:
Arrows are a new abstract view of computation, defined by John
Hughes [Hug00]. They serve much the same purpose as monads --
providing a common structure for libraries -- but are more general.

arrows: Arrow classes and transformers.

Several classes that extend the Arrow class, and some transformers
that implement or lift these classes.

http://www.haskell.org/arrows
